test.command: go list ./internal/... | grep -v 'github.com/yusefmosiah/cogent/internal/adapters/codex' | grep -v 'github.com/yusefmosiah/cogent/internal/mcpserver' | xargs env GOMAXPROCS=4 go test

blockingIssues.description: The live transport regression still only uses helper selection. `TestStreamableHTTPHandlerIntegration` never invokes `handler.ServeHTTP` or `session.CallTool`, so the production StreamableHTTP path is not exercised end to end.

The new live transport provenance test repeats the helper-only pattern. It constructs a handler but still never drives the actual live transport path, so VAL-SUPERVISOR-003 remains unproven on the production route.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require live StreamableHTTP regressions to invoke `handler.ServeHTTP` or `session.CallTool` when a feature claims live transport coverage; helper-level request/server selection is not sufficient.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviewed fix feature and its predecessor both stopped at `GetServerForRequest` / `GetServerInstance`, leaving the actual transport path untested.

rejectedObservations.observation: The test comments describe an 'actual StreamableHTTP handler path' even though the code still only exercises helper-level request selection.
rejectedObservations.details: This is the same underlying transport-coverage gap already captured in the blocking issues and guidance update.
